title: Document Controller
description:
Gemkom Engineering and Machinery Filial is a recently established branch of Gemkom, a leading Turkish engineering and manufacturing company operating across various sectors within the steel industry.
  
We are proud to contribute to the project initiated by Stegra AB (H2 Green Steel), establishing a steel production facility in Norra Svartbyn, Boden. This facility will utilize an innovative, near fossil-free production method based on hydrogen gas.
  
Gemkom’s scope of work includes the installation of machinery integral to the steel production process, encompassing mechanical, electrical, and plumbing disciplines.
  
We now want to employ an Document Controller. The work tasks may include, but are not limited to;
  
Position Summary
  
The Document Controller is responsible for managing, organizing, and maintaining project documentation to ensure accuracy, accessibility, and compliance with company procedures and contractual requirements. The role supports efficient information flow between project teams, clients, and contractors.
  
  

**Key Responsibilities:**  

1. Document Management
  
* Maintain and control project documentation including drawings, technical documents, contracts, and correspondence.
* Ensure documents are properly registered, classified, and stored in the document management system (DMS).
* Track document revisions and maintain version control.


2. Distribution and Communication
  
* Distribute documents to relevant stakeholders according to project distribution matrices.
* Ensure that the latest approved versions of documents are available to project teams.
* Coordinate document submissions between contractors, consultants, and clients.


3. Document Control System Administration
  
* Manage electronic document management systems such as SharePoint, AutoCAD Cloud, Omega365, or similar platforms.
* Ensure proper naming conventions, metadata, and document numbering systems are followed.


4. Meeting Documentation and Minutes
  
* Attend key project meetings involving major stakeholders when required.
* Record accurate meeting minutes, including decisions, action items, and responsibilities.
* Prepare and distribute meeting minutes to relevant stakeholders within the required timeframe.
* Maintain a structured archive of meeting minutes and action trackers for project reference.


5. Tracking and Reporting
  
* Maintain document registers, transmittal logs, and submission trackers.
* Monitor document approval workflows and follow up on overdue responses.
* Prepare periodic document status reports for project management.


6. Quality and Compliance
  
* Ensure document control procedures comply with company policies and project requirements.
* Support audits and quality checks related to documentation.


**Qualifications and Requirements (not mandatory):**  

* Diploma or a Bachelor’s degree.
* At least 2 years of experience in document control and relevant process management.

title: Cloud Network specialist | SEB, Sweden
description:
Join our innovative team to shape the future of cloud networking within SEB’s progressive tech landscape. You’ll drive network development for our GCP and Azure environment, making a tangible impact in a collaborative, agile setting.



SEB is more than just a bank with an IT function. We are a progressive tech company powered by innovation and fuelled by collaboration. From COBOL to AI and everything in between, we offer you an impressive tech stack and several technology paths. Together we’re innovating for a sustainable society and building the tech arena of the future, a welcoming space for everyone. Are you ready to take on a new challenge? If so, you’ll be interested in this opportunity!


### **About the role:**



As a Cloud Network Specialist, you will primarily manage network related topics within our Core & Cloud team, developing and maintaining its infrastructure, including Network Security and automation. You’ll balance maintenance with new development in an agile framework, continuously striving for process improvements, standardization, and automation for speed and precision. We value your commitment to sharing knowledge, ensuring secure solutions, and minimizing overlapping functionality. You will also apply analytical thinking to identify and resolve complex technical challenges and collaborate flexibly across virtual teams.



In this role work on-call during nights and weekends every 4-6 weeks and from time-to-time maintenance work outside office hours will be included.



### **To thrive in this role, we believe you have:**


* Proficiency in cloud networking across GCP and Azure environments.
* Advanced understanding of network technology disciplines including IP Routing, Switching, Routing Protocols, WAN, Firewall, and Packet Analysis.
* Strong experience with automation tools like Terraform/ARM and Ansible, plus working in Gitlab or similar.
* Knowledge in programming using Python, Bash, PowerShell or similar scripting languages.
* Excellent collaboration skills, the ability to work independently and confidently, and a commitment to analytical thinking for problem-solving.
### **What we offer:**

* Agile and modern ways of working


* A flat hierarchy and openness to share ideas, opinions and points of views


* Extensive training and learning opportunities


* Hybrid work model, meaning remote work is possible up to 2 days/week


* Innovative company at the forefront of technology


**Do you want to be part of SEB?**



It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.



Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today via the link in this ad, but no later than 2026-03-30. If you have questions about the position, please contact Ronnie Andersson, ronnie.andersson@seb.se, note that we will not process applications via email.



Please be aware that our final candidates undergo background checks, a process that includes for example identity control, verification of qualifications, credit checks, company engagements and history of crime. In some cases, we also apply random drug checks. During employment, employees within SEB can also expect to undergo recurring background checks.



SEB Sweden has a redeployment responsibility, which means that this position might be covered by internal redeployment.
company_description: More than 15.000 people work at SEB. Working in SEB means being part of an exciting journey, driven by digital transformation

title: Content and Design Specialist
description:
**Företagsbeskrivning** *Bosch Thermoteknik AB i Tranås utvecklar, tillverkar och säljer avancerade värmepumpar som tar tillvara på energi från naturens resurser – berg, vatten och luft.*


I Tranås hittar du både vår produktionsenhet och vårt kompetens- och utvecklingscenter, där vi ständigt strävar efter att ligga i framkant. Här skapar vi smarta lösningar som förenklar vardagen, bidrar till en grönare framtid och ger våra kunder trygghet och komfort.  

Vi tror på teknik som gör skillnad – teknik som är **Invented for life**.

 **Jobbeskrivning**  

Är du en webb- och digital expert med känsla för visuell kommunikation? Vi söker dig som vill utveckla våra digitala kanaler och visuella uttryck. Rollen är bred, operativ och kreativ, med ansvar för webb, digitala medier och tryckt material. Du blir en nyckelspelare i vår projekt intensiva miljö, samarbetar tätt med marknad, produkt och byråer för att säkerställa högkvalitativt webbinnehåll, grafisk produktion och digital utveckling i alla våra kanaler.

 **Kvalifikationer** **Vi söker dig som har:**

* Mycket goda kunskaper i **Adobe Creative Cloud** (Photoshop, Illustrator och InDesign)
* Erfarenhet av **webbpublicering och CMS**
* God förståelse för **design för både digitala och tryckta medier**
* Mycket goda kunskaper i **svenska och engelska**, både i tal och skrift

**Det är en fördel om du även har erfarenhet av:**

* Examen inom **grafisk design, digital design eller motsvarande**
* **Grundläggande SEO-kunskaper** och förmåga att tillämpa dessa i praktiken
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta med **sociala medier för marknadsföring och kommunikation**
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta i CMS-system, gärna **Episerver / Optimizely**
* Analys av webbdata och digitala kanalers performance

**Personliga egenskaper**


Vi lägger stor vikt vid vem du är som person, därav ser vi att du:

* Har ett starkt **formsinne och öga för detaljer**
* Är **organiserad och strukturerad**
* Har lätt för att **lära dig nya verktyg och arbetssätt**
* Är **idérik och lösningsorienterad**
* Har en **analytisk förmåga** och kan omsätta insikter till förbättringar
* Trivs med att arbeta både självständigt och tillsammans med andra

  

**Ytterligare information**  

Hos oss får du möjlighet att arbeta i en roll där **design, digital utveckling och kommunikation möts**, och där du får bidra till att utveckla våra kanaler framåt – både digitalt och i tryck, tillsammans med kollegor och externa partners.

**Välkommen till Tranås – hjärtat av Bosch Home Comfort i Sverige**


Tranås, vackert beläget vid den idylliska sjön Sommen och omgivet av skogar och naturreservat, är hem för Bosch Thermoteknik i Sverige. Här arbetar över 600 medarbetare med tillverkning och forskning & utveckling av innovativa uppvärmningslösningar för en hållbar framtid.


På Bosch bryr vi oss om dig, vår verksamhet och miljön.  

Vi erbjuder mer än ett jobb – vi hjälper dig att växa i din karriär och som person. Livet handlar om balans, och vi stöttar dina mål, oavsett om det gäller nya utmaningar, utveckling eller mer tid för familj och fritid.


Bli en del av en arbetsplats som värdesätter individualitet, öppenhet och tillit, där du kan vara dig själv och inspireras av olika perspektiv från hela världen.  

Kom till Tranås och var med och forma framtidens energilösningar – på en arbetsplats där innovation möter livskvalitet.  

Här hittar du inte bara ett jobb – utan en livsstil. #LikeABosch

**Compensation and Benefits**

* Competitive salary, Pension plans, health insurance, and other benefits according to the collective bargin.
* Flexible working hours and **hybrid work options** (depending on role).
* Employee discounts on Bosch products.
company_description: Bosch is a leading IoT company and global supplier of technology and services in the Mobility Solutions, Industrial Technology, Consumer Goods, and Energy and Building Technology sectors.

title: Senior Solution Sales Executive | Data & AI
description:
**We help the world run better**  

At SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed. **What You'll Do**  

  

The Solution Sales Executive (SSE) for SAP (Sweden/Denmark) partners with the end-to-end account owner to drive solution-specific sales motions, bring in domain expertise to solve customer business challenges, and grow the customer’s SAP footprint through renewal and expansion to support long-term customer success.* Generate demand, manage pipeline, and close opportunities
* Develop opportunity plans containing compelling solution value propositions
* Conduct White Space analysis to identify growth opportunities
* Work with the wider account team on sales campaigns
* Manage customer relationships at the solution area/buying center level
* Progress opportunities for move to cloud/expand footprint accounts or accounts that are new to the solution area
* Utilize deep knowledge of how companies operate, business models, strategies, and end-to-end business processes
* Stay informed about SAP’s competition and value drivers
* Leverage SAP’s comprehensive team of experts and industry knowledge to effectively address customer needs
* Build customer participation in relevant SAP communities, programs, and events
* Facilitate collaboration with the partner ecosystem

 **What You Bring*** Experience selling, Database, Data Management, Analytics and/or Technology solutions to large Enterprises.
* Experience in selling Artificial Intelligence, Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS), Process Automation, and LCNC (Low Code/No Code) solutions.
* Management of large, complex sales cycles with expectations to achieve quota.
* Strong communication and presentation skills with the ability to interact at the CXO level. Experience hunting for new business and expanding our business relationships in existing accounts.
* Business value and positioning of Platform as Service solutions like SAP BTP, MuleSoft, etc.
* Ability to forecast/ manage sales cycles, and successfully close new business.
* Proven problem-resolution, communication, planning & organizing, and execution skills.
* Business development, prospecting, and presentation skills.
* A proven track record of exceeding sales objectives and territory/account development.
* Bachelor’s Degree

 **Meet Your Team*** Demonstrate and sell the business benefits and value proposition of SAP Database, Data Management and Analytics, and Technology solutions.
* Collaborate with support resources to maximize SAP BTP revenue.
* Identify customer business pains and challenges and articulate those challenges into SAP solutions.
* Identify areas of innovation within the client base, and then drive resources to prove the innovation and its business value for the customer.
* Develop new business sales opportunities in new accounts.
* Manage several complex sales opportunities simultaneously.
* Win complex sales deals in a competitive environment to achieve sales targets.
* Maintain broad and deep knowledge of SAP BTP portfolio and industry trends.

 **Location**
Stockholm or Copenhagen **Bring out your best**  

SAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end-to-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. As a cloud company with two hundred million users and more than one hundred thousand employees worldwide, we are purpose-driven and future-focused, with a highly collaborative team ethic and commitment to personal development. Whether connecting global industries, people, or platforms, we help ensure every challenge gets the solution it deserves. At SAP, you can bring out your best.
  

  

**We win with inclusion**  

SAP’s culture of inclusion, focus on health and well-being, and flexible working models help ensure that everyone – regardless of background – feels included and can run at their best. At SAP, we believe we are made stronger by the unique capabilities and qualities that each person brings to our company, and we invest in our employees to inspire confidence and help everyone realize their full potential. We ultimately believe in unleashing all talent and creating a better world.  

  

SAP is committed to the values of Equal Employment Opportunity and provides accessibility accommodations to applicants with physical and/or mental disabilities. If you are interested in applying for employment with SAP and are in need of accommodation or special assistance to navigate our website or to complete your application, please send an e-mail with your request to Recruiting Operations Team: Careers@sap.com.  

  

For SAP employees: Only permanent roles are eligible for the SAP Employee Referral Program, according to the eligibility rules set in the SAP Referral Policy. Specific conditions may apply for roles in Vocational Training.  

  

Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their age, race, religion, national origin, ethnicity, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, et al), sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or disability, in compliance with applicable federal, state, and local legal requirements.  

  

Successful candidates might be required to undergo a background verification with an external vendor. **AI Usage in the Recruitment Process**

title: SAP Cloud ERP Finance Architect
description:
**We help the world run better**  

At SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed. **YOUR TEAM**


We are experienced team of architects located across several Nordic countries. Our team works both with on customer assignments and with our sales colleagues to position viable approaches that support our customers’ situations. We help customers get approaches implemented and realize value. We conceptualize solutions, but we also enjoy getting our hands into the system. Together as a team, we make miracles. We are looking for and open-minded and flexible colleague who will complement our team of highly esteemed experts to make SAP Cloud ERP successful. Apply now, and let’s start discussion on how you can build your own track in SAP.

 **ROLE DESCRIPTION**


We are looking for a finance architect for SAP Cloud ERP. Your focus is on understanding end-to-end finance processes and principles, with deep finance domain expertise. You have solid experience with SAP ERP implementations, preferably in the public cloud, and a strong functional background. You listen carefully to uncover customer pain points and requirements, and you drive those requirements into design and delivery. You support and guide stakeholders to make sound decisions, steering them toward fit-to-standard solutions, SAP best practices, and relevant scope items. You lead finance process discussions and translate requirements into clear solution options and adoption roadmaps. You enjoy leading and guiding with a gentle, professional manner, collaborating internally and externally across the ecosystem to accelerate adoption.


You can be located in any of the Nordic Countries. Relocation is not supported.

 **As an SAP Cloud ERP and public cloud-focused finance architect, you will**

* Master the public cloud project lifecycle.
* Leverage a minimum of five years’ finance domain expertise and either at least two end-to-end SAP Cloud ERP public cloud implementations or two years of hands-on public cloud delivery experience, to guide customers confidently.
* Use a public-cloud architect approach to help customers design and adopt best practice, end-to-end solution concepts.
* Guide customers to maintain clean-core principles in SAP Cloud ERP, prioritizing fit-to-standard, standard scope items, and SAP Best Practices content, and using approved extensibility instead of core modifications.
* Support customers in automating their finance processes, leveraging intelligent capabilities and embedded AI within SAP Cloud ERP.
* Actively participate in delivery to demonstrate how modern SAP solutions can be adopted.
* Collaborate effectively with SAP partners.
* Manage internal teams and collaborate with partners during the solution approach definition process.
* Build trusted-advisor relationships with customer stakeholders.
* Partner with Sales and Account teams to present the benefits and value proposition of SAP finance solutions and architecture.
* Work with Sales on demand creation by attending customer or prospect meetings to present SAP services capabilities and delivery approach.
* Prepare system demos and discuss processes and functional features.

 **YOUR PROFILE**

* You are used to leading customer discussions and have an approach that moves customers forward.
* You can take a helicopter view to see the overall architecture.
* You are eager to deep-dive when a concept requires a closer look.
* You use systems to prove that concepts are working.
* You can communicate easily with developers and management.
* Business communication in both English and the regional language is needed.

title: Customer Support Engineer
description:
We are looking for a Customer Support Engineer to join our global Operate Customer Support Team. In this role, you will support our digital products used in mining operations and contribute to delivering worldclass customer service together with Customer Centres, R&D, and platform teams.  


**Your Mission**

As a Customer Support Engineer, you provide high quality 2nd Level Support for selected customer installations. You monitor systems proactively, troubleshoot technical issues, handle support tickets, and maintain close communication with Epiroc Customer Centres and customers.  



You will be an important part in keeping Epiroc customers happy by participating in support meetings and guide customers to use Epiroc digital products towards a safer and more efficient operation.  



You assist delivery projects through installations, training, initial deployments, and occasional customer visits. You also improve internal troubleshooting documentation and support 1st Level when needed.  

This role includes participation in the 24/7 on call rotation every 8th week to ensure continuous service availability.  



Additionally, you may contribute to secondary areas such as maintaining support documentation, assisting with mobile app releases, supporting advanced integrations and product optimisations, handling hardware warranty tasks, performing installation tests, maintaining demo environments, and preparing maintenance release notes.  


**Your Profile**

You are a technically strong and customer-oriented professional with experience in Linux server administration (Ubuntu/RedHat), databases (MariaDB/MongoDB), networking (DNS, certificates, firewalls), and troubleshooting complex systems.  



You are comfortable using support tools like Jira, Nagios, and related platforms, and you understand the basics of cluster and cloud technologies.  



You communicate clearly in English and enjoy working directly with customers and cross functional teams.  


You work methodically, solve problems efficiently, and escalate when needed. You manage your time well and handle multiple tasks with structure and prioritization.  



Collaboration comes naturally to you, and you contribute to a positive team environment by supporting colleagues and embracing new ideas.  


You bring a proactive mindset, focus on improvements, and thrive in a dynamic and evolving setting.  


**Location and other**

This position is located in Luleå, Sweden.  


We offer a hybrid workplace, providing flexibility and promoting a healthier balance between professional and personal life. Occasional travel is a possibility if desired.  


**Why Epiroc?**

By joining our team, you’ll make a big difference in the energy transition. At Epiroc, we take pride in being passionate innovators, driving the change toward a brighter future for both people and the planet. Guided by our values of Collaboration, Commitment, and Innovation, we foster a culture of trust, growth, and lasting impact.



 To support your journey, we offer a competitive Total Rewards Package, including:


* Global career opportunities
* Learning & Development programs
* Benefits package, including variable compensation (bonus)
* Flexible work culture promoting balance and well-being
* Global Parental Leave Policy
* Community involvement

  


**Application and contacts**

We encourage you to submit your application through our online career site as soon as possible, but no later than **April 3rd, 2026**.  


We are committed to a thorough recruitment process, including interviews, reference checks and assessments. To ensure a safe working environment, we conduct identity checks and drug and alcohol screening. Our process is designed to be fair and inclusive; you can expect transparent communication and a balanced evaluation of your skills and experience.  



We review applications on a rolling basis. Please note that, due to the current regulations, we will only consider applications received through our system and not via email or social media.  



For question about the position, please contact hiring manager Daniel Marklund, Team Manager Costumer Support (daniel.marklund@epiroc.com)  



For questions about the hiring process, please contact Dana Galova, Recruitment Specialist (dana.galova@epiroc.com)  


**Other**

In case a candidate from a different country applies and is successful, local terms and conditions will apply.  


We have already made our choices regarding advertising channels and consulting providers, and kindly request not to be contacted regarding such inquiries.

  

**It all starts with people.** The world needs metals and minerals for the energy transition and our cities and infrastructure must be developed to serve a growing population. To succeed, we need to speed up the shift towards more sustainable mining and construction industries. We at Epiroc accelerate this transformation, together with customers and business partners in more than 150 countries, by developing and providing innovative and safe equipment, digital solutions, and aftermarket support.
All new thinkers are welcome. We are looking for those who want to develop, grow, and dare to think new. In Epiroc we attract, develop, and retain diverse talent valuing authenticity and unique perspectives, driving our spirit of innovation. We foster an inclusive culture where diversity isn't just a goal but a part of our values and way of working. This is how we do business for a sustainable future. Learn more at www.epiroc.com

title: Distribution Sales Manager
description:
**Overview**
------------


At NetApp, we have a history of helping customers turn challenges into business opportunities. As part of our sales organization, your role will require a strategic blend of technical acumen and charismatic client engagement, ensuring that every partnership is nurtured towards it maximum potential. As a pivotal link between NetApp and our clients, your contributions will directly influence the growth and direction of our department, making a lasting impact on the organization's success. This is more than a job—it's a chance to be part of a team that values innovation, supports professional growth, and celebrates shared victories.
  
**Own Every Moment at NetApp**


At NetApp, your ideas power innovation. We lead in intelligent data infrastructure—delivering unified storage, integrated data services, and solutions that help organizations unlock the full potential of their data, from AI to multicloud.


Ready to innovate and contribute to our path to $10B? Here, you'll collaborate with passionate teams, tackle real-world challenges, and see your impact in how customers transform and grow. If you're ready to bring curiosity, creativity, and drive to every moment, NetApp is where your journey begins.


**Job Summary**
---------------


The Distribution Sales Manager will be responsible for the country’s channel strategy in engaging, motivating and growing our partners’ business in NetApp. They will be driving sales of NetApp's products and services, meeting sales goals, and promoting a preference for NetApp among partners through effective strategy and execution.

**Job Responsibilities**
------------------------

* Develop and maintain a multi-year sales strategy and business plan and tracking the success of selected Partners and the distributors.
* Designing plans and tracking activities with distributors to build pipeline generation plans to maintain consistent revenue every quarter.
* Manage the executive engagement between the partner and corresponding parts of the NetApp sales organization
* Plan, execute and track tactical activities to support bookings and pipeline generation
* Drive and support Partner to develop services practices and specializations
* Orchestrates enablement of Partner sales and technical teams in line with Program requirements and Solutions GTM
* Plans and manages Incentive Headcount resources
* Help Partners get the right readiness to drive solution and services sales
* Activate and run company initiatives and programs at Partners
* Drive Partner forecast accuracy and overall health
* Implement continues Installed Base expansion and retention strategies and tactics
* Collaborate with Partner’s and NetApp Commercial team (CTR/CAM) to develop Partner Driven Commercial business
* Collaborate with enterprise- and commercial account teams and Partner to create and execute pursuits
**Job Requirements**
--------------------

* A minimum of 8 years of experience working with Channel, Alliance, or ISV partners, along with a solid understanding of storage and infrastructure.
* Ability to effectively interface and communicate at many levels within Partner companies' organization
* Aptitude to negotiate and resolve complex situations/opportunities to a positive outcome while managing conflicts to maintain ecosystem of internal or external relationships
* Ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams
* Tactical skills to builds a network outside to support business needs
* Good understanding of and network within the industry ecosystem (Partners, competition etc.)
* Good knowledge of Cloud technologies and business models
* Ability to travel 2-3 days per week

At NetApp, we embrace a hybrid working environment designed to strengthen connection, collaboration, and culture for all employees. This means that most roles will have some level of in-office and/or in-person expectations, which will be shared during the recruitment process.


**Equal Opportunity Employer:**


NetApp is firmly committed to Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and to compliance with all federal, state and local laws that prohibit employment discrimination based on age, race, color, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, religion, disability or genetic information, pregnancy, protected veteran status, and any other protected classification.

**Why You'll Thrive at NetApp**


At NetApp, you won't wait for the perfect moment—you'll make it. The early planning, the extra thought, the bold idea that turns good into great: That's how our people operate and how we continue to push the boundaries of data infrastructure.


NetApp is the trusted partner for organizations transforming data into opportunity. As the only enterprise-grade storage service natively embedded in Google Cloud, AWS, and Microsoft Azure, we empower customers to run everything from traditional workloads to enterprise AI with unmatched performance, resilience, and security.

**Our culture**


We celebrate mold breakers, bold thinkers, and problem solvers. We reward initiative, impact, and ownership. We provide flexibility so you can balance professional ambition with your personal life. Here, differences are not just welcomed—they drive everything we do.


If you're ready to innovate, rise to the challenge, and own every moment - make your next move your best one. now.


**Submitting an application**  

  

To ensure a streamlined and fair hiring process for all candidates, our team only reviews applications submitted through our company website. This practice allows us to track, assess, and respond to applicants efficiently. Emailing our employees, recruiters, or Human Resources personnel directly will not influence your application.


  
**Our values**
--------------


Put the customer at the center. Care for each other and our communities. Think and act like owners. Build belonging every day. Embrace a growth mindset.


  
**Benefits**
------------


### **Volunteer time off**


40 hours of paid volunteer time each year.
### **Well-being**


Employee Assistance Program, fitness, and mental health resources to help employees be their best.
### **Time away**


Paid time off for vacation and to recharge.
company_description: If you run toward knowledge and problem-solving, join us.

title: ​​Product Management Director​
description:
Join Enea’s Network Solutions team as Product Manager Director, where you will shape the strategy and direction of our Core Network Applications portfolio in a high-impact leadership role. Reporting to the Chief Product, Technology, Strategy and Marketing Officer, you will work at the centre of product, technology and market insight to deliver solutions that meet customer needs and support business growth.

**Role and Key Responsibilities**  

This role is based in Stockholm and plays a central part in shaping the strategy, direction, and performance of Enea’s Core Network Applications portfolio. As Product Management Director, you will translate customer needs, market insight, and business priorities into clear product plans, while working closely with cross-functional teams to deliver strong commercial and technical outcomes.

 **Key responsibilities include:**

* Define the product strategy for the Core Network Applications portfolio, using customer insight, market analysis, and business objectives to set clear direction.
* Define and prioritise product requirements, ensuring customer needs and commercial priorities are translated into actionable plans.
* Own the product roadmap and backlog, maintaining alignment across strategy, user stories, and acceptance criteria.
* Partner closely with engineering, marketing, and sales teams to deliver releases on time and within scope.
* Monitor technical performance, financial performance, and customer feedback, using these insights to guide product improvements and iteration.
* Represent the customer perspective in decision-making, ensuring solutions address key market needs and deliver clear value.
* Oversee the full product lifecycle, identifying new opportunities and balancing customer needs, technical feasibility, and business goals.

**Qualifications**, **Key Skills, and Competence**  

  



**Required Qualifications**

* Relevant third-level qualification in business, engineering, telecommunications, or a related field.
* At least 8 years of experience in product management, or a similar role, within B2B technology markets, ideally in telecoms.
* Strong experience working with telecom and enterprise customers, and with sales teams, to understand complex technical and commercial requirements.
* Proven ability to translate customer and market needs into clear product requirements, roadmaps, and measurable outcomes.
* Strong technical background in networking, including technologies and the wider ecosystem.
* Experience working closely with engineering, sales, and marketing teams to support product delivery and go-to-market activity.
* Additional training or certification in product management or agile methodologies would be an advantage.

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Additional training or certification in product management or agile methodologies.
* Experience in telecoms or other complex B2B technology markets.
* Strong knowledge of Core Network Applications, including 3GPP, cloud native technologies, and artificial intelligence.

**What can Enea offer you?**  

At Enea, we strive to create an environment where you can thrive both professionally and personally. We offer:

* **Flexible hybrid work:** A modern work model that supports a healthy work–life balance.
* **Collaborative environment:** A dynamic, globally connected team culture that fosters engagement in cybersecurity initiatives and cross-regional projects.
* **Inclusive culture:** A diverse and inclusive workplace that values your voice and promotes mutual respect and collaboration.
* **Competitive compensation:** A competitive salary package complemented by region-specific benefits.
* **Ongoing learning and career development:** Continuous learning opportunities and clearly defined career paths to support your professional growth across the organization.


Enea’s Network Solutions business develops software that helps mobile operators connect, optimise, and secure their networks. Its scope spans critical telecom functions, including core network applications and data management, with a clear focus on helping customers improve performance, support service innovation, and deliver secure, reliable connectivity at scale.

**About Enea**:


Enea is a global specialist in advanced telecom and cybersecurity software, with a vision to make the world's communications safer and more efficient. Through continuous innovation, our solutions help customers optimize, secure, and monetize their global communication services, enabling seamless connections between people, businesses, and connected devices worldwide. We serve 300 customers across 100+ countries, including telecom operators, cloud communication providers, governments, and cybersecurity vendors. More than 3 billion people rely on Enea technologies every day. Headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden, Enea is publicly listed on NASDAQ Stockholm. www.enea.com


  
Department
Technology, Product and Marketing

Product Manager
Locations
Stockholm, Sweden

title: Sales Enablement & Training Manager
description:
#### **About Net Insight**


Net Insight is a leading media technology and network solutions company enabling broadcasters, service providers, and enterprises to deliver the highest-quality live video, data, and cloud services worldwide.


Headquartered in Stockholm, Net Insight has long been recognised for innovation in synchronisation, media transport, and cloud-based production workflows. Customers include many of the world’s most prominent broadcasters, telcos, and sports rights holders.


Following a strategic realignment five years ago the product offering has gone through significant modernization which has seen the Net Insight business grow significantly. The company is now from a solid platform entering an exciting new phase, continuing to evolve into a market- and product-led business. All with the aim of continuing the growth of the business.


#### **About the role**


We are opening the opportunity for a **Sales Enablement & Training Manager** to take ownership of our global enablement and training framework across Sales, Partners, and Customer Success. This role is central to how we scale knowledge, align messaging, and ensure consistent understanding and adoption of Net Insight’s media transport solutions globally – including Nimbra appliances, IP media workflows, and Nimbra Edge.


You will be responsible for structuring, developing, and continuously improving our global sales enablement content, onboarding programmes, and certification paths. The role combines commercial understanding, technical fluency, and strong facilitation skills, and requires close collaboration with Product Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, Product & Engineering, and Regional teams.

#### **Key responsibilities**

* Own and govern the global sales enablement framework (master sales decks, playbooks, talk tracks, demo guides, objection handling)
* Lead global sales onboarding, including curriculum, learning paths, and certification steps
* Build and maintain scalable digital training programmes for internal teams and partners
* Deliver live training sessions (virtual and in-person) to commercial and technical audiences
* Develop training content covering Net Insight’s media workflows, services, and operational concepts
* Own structure, taxonomy, and governance of the global enablement & training content library
* Track training engagement, completion, and adoption, and continuously improve based on feedback

#### **Qualifications & experience**


We believe you will succeed in this role if you have a strong combination of commercial understanding, technical credibility, and a passion for enablement and learning:

**Experience & background**

* Several years of experience in roles such as Sales Enablement, Pre-Sales / Solutions Engineering, Technical Training, Product Marketing, or similar
* Experience working with media technology, broadcast, IP contribution, cloud-based workflows, telco/media networks, or other complex B2B technology environments
* Proven experience delivering training and enablement to technical audiences (e.g. network operations, MCR/TOC, contribution or field specialists)
* Experience working in global, distributed organisations and collaborating across regions and functions

**Technical & functional competence**

* Solid understanding of media transport workflows, IP-based contribution, redundancy principles, service provisioning, and cloud-based media workflows (or strong ability and motivation to build this competence quickly)
* Ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear, structured, and practical training and sales-facing content
* Strong skills in building structured frameworks, documentation, playbooks, and repeatable enablement processes
* Experience creating learning paths, onboarding programmes, and certification structures
* Comfort using digital learning platforms, content management systems (e.g. SharePoint, LMS, training hubs), and basic video or tutorial formats

**Communication & ways of working**

* Confident communicator and facilitator, comfortable leading training sessions and workshops for both commercial and technical audiences
* Strong collaboration skills and ability to work closely with Sales, Product Marketing, Product, Engineering, CSM, and Regional teams
* High level of structure and attention to detail, with the ability to manage multiple initiatives in parallel
* Comfortable working with global teams and different levels of technical and commercial maturity
* Proactive and improvement-driven mindset – you identify enablement gaps and turn them into concrete actions

#### **What’s in it for you**


This is a high-impact, cross-functional role with strong visibility and the opportunity to shape how we enable and upskill our global commercial organisation and partners. You will have real influence on how our solutions are positioned, understood, and successfully delivered to customers worldwide.

#### **Practicalities**

**Location:** UK or Sweden  

**Reports to:** CMO  

**Contract:** Permanent, full-time


Please contact recruiting manager Yaya Selva at yaya.selva@netinsight.net with any questions about the role.

**ABOUT NET INSIGHT**


*Net Insight (Nasdaq: NETI B) provides the highest performing, most open video transport and media cloud technology for content providers as the industry standard for flexibility and service across live contribution, distribution and remote production media workflows.*

*For over 25 years, the world’s leading content owners, broadcasters, production companies, service providers and enterprises have trusted Net Insight’s Emmy Award winning Nimbra technology to guarantee media delivery. Today, Net Insight partners with hundreds of customers in over 70 countries to ensure media flows across managed and unmanaged IP networks, and the cloud – from anywhere, to everywhere. It enables customers to get the best from any mix of virtualized, cloud and IP technology and is the only platform to support all the major industry standards, protocols and clouds.*

 *Welcome to Net Insight, where* ***innovation****,* ***collaboration****, and* ***trust*** *are the cornerstones of our success. As one team, we are committed to delivering value to our customers, contributing to the growth and success of our company. Together we create a dynamic and positive workplace where everyone is valued, engaged and empowered to make an impact*

title: Cloud Infrastructure Engineer
description:
Vår uppdragsgivare söker nu en systemingenjör som drivs av att skapa skalbarhet och robusthet där kraven på säkerhet och tillgänglighet är som allra högst. Här får du chansen att vara arkitekten bakom den underliggande kraften i ett system som verkar i en unik och tekniskt utmanande marin domän.

### **Rollen och ditt uppdrag**


Som Cloud Infrastructure Engineer ansvarar du för design, utveckling och livscykelhantering av en modern IT-infrastruktur. Du bygger plattformen som möjliggör nästa generations marina lösningar.

* **Plattformsbygge:** Du designar och underhåller en infrastruktur baserad på OpenStack.
* **Infrastructure as Code (IaC):** Du automatiserar provisionering och konfiguration med verktyg som Terraform och Puppet.
* **Integration:** Du säkerställer att komplexa mjukvarukomponenter integreras sömlöst i den marina plattformen.
* **Framtidssäkring:** Du utvecklar arkitektur som möter extrema säkerhetskrav och är flexibel nog för framtidens teknologier och ramverk.

### **Vem är du?**


Vi söker dig som opererar på en hög komplexitetsnivå och har en djup teknisk expertis. För att lyckas i rollen ser vi att din profil matchar följande:

* **Teknisk Expertis:** Djup erfarenhet av molninfrastruktur, containerisering och virtualisering, med särskilt fokus på **OpenStack**.
* **Automations-fokus:** Du är en förespråkare för IaC och behärskar moderna automationsverktyg obehindrat.
* **Systemförståelse:** Du har en gedigen förståelse för hela stacken – från Network och Storage till Compute.
* **Meriterande:** Vi ser positivt på erfarenhet av arbete i **SAFe** och miljöer med höga säkerhetskrav (säkerhetsklassning kan förekomma).


Vårt **Talent Acquisition-team** ser gärna att du som söker har goda referenser samt ett välarbetat CV som specificerar tidigare erfarenheter och utbildning inom området. Vi intervjuar löpande, så visa gärna ditt intresse så snart som möjligt. Vi välkomnar sökande med olika bakgrunder och erfarenheter, eftersom vi vet att mångfald berikar och stärker vårt arbete. Vi tar endast emot ansökningar via vårt system. Vid frågor, vänligen kontakta maria@linden-it.se.

title: Informationssäkerhetsspecialist
description:
Sista ansökningsdatum: 16 april

Är du vår nya informationssäkerhetsspecialist?


**Om tjänsten**
---------------



I rollen som Informationssäkerhetsspecialist, i Svenska kraftnäts team för informationssäkerhet, kommer du att arbeta brett med styrning av säkerhet och tredjepartsrisk. Ditt arbete bidrar direkt till att säkerställa att Svenska kraftnät har rätt strukturer, processer och kontrollmiljö för att möta både dagens och framtidens krav.



Du kommer bl.a. att ansvara för att:


* Vidareutveckla och förvalta vårt Ledningssystem för Informationssäkerhet (LIS) samt formulera interna säkerhetskrav.
* Stötta verksamheten som rådgivare i implementering av säkerhetsåtgärder och tolkning av krav.
* Följa upp efterlevnad av externa krav och interna riktlinjer.
* Vara ett generellt säkerhetsstöd genom rådgivning, riskbedömningar och initiativ för att stärka säkerheten.
* Vara utpekad specialist för tredjepartsrisk och vidareutveckla vårt arbetssätt för att bedöma, granska och följa upp leverantörers säkerhet.
* Genomföra granskningar av våra leverantörers säkerhet.


Här får du stort utrymme att påverka arbetssätt, metodik och prioriteringar – och du blir en nyckelperson i SVK:s säkerhetsstyrning.  

Genom din gedigna erfarenhet inom informationssäkerhet, riskhantering och tredjepartsstyrning blir du en viktig specialistresurs för SVK och en del av vårt långsiktiga strategiska säkerhetsarbete.



Enhet Informations- och cybersäkerhet består av cirka 15 anställda i två team.  

Enheten tillhör avdelningen Säkerhet och beredskap. Avdelningen har drygt 100 medarbetare.



Tjänsten är tillsvidare och placerad på vårt huvudkontor i Sundbyberg.


**Om dig**
----------


**Skallkrav:**



Personliga egenskaper*:*  

I denna rekrytering kommer mycket stor vikt läggas vid dina personliga egenskaper.
  

För att lyckas i rollen behöver du vara strukturerad, analytisk och kommunikativ. Du är bra på att omsätta komplexa krav till tydliga och praktiska lösningar och trivs i en roll där du samarbetar med många olika intressenter, inklusive våra leverantörer.



Du är även noggrann, analytisk och resultatinriktad.
  

Sist men inte minst är du trygg och prestigelös, vilket bland annat innebär att du kan stå upp för dina beslut även när de ifrågasätts, samtidigt som du kan ompröva dem när så behövs.



Utbildning:
  

  

Relevant akademisk utbildning inom informationssäkerhet, informationsteknik eller erfarenheter/kvalifikationer som vi bedömer likvärdiga.
  

  

Du har även:


* God kunskap om etablerade ramverk som NIST (CSF, SP 800), ISO 27001, ISO 31000 eller motsvarande.
* God förståelse för riskhantering, särskilt inom Cloud och TPRM (Third-Party Risk Management).
* Förmåga att förstå och bedöma tekniska säkerhetslösningar, t.ex. inom molntjänster (AWS/Azure/GCP), nätverksarkitektur och åtkomstkontroll.
* Erfarenhet av arbete med informationssäkerhet och TPRM.
* Erfarenhet och vana att arbeta med lagar och regelverk.
* Du behärskar svenska mycket väl i tal och skrift, då du kommer att driva processer, skriva styrande dokument och leda dialoger med både
  

tekniska och icke-tekniska grupper.


**Meriterande:**


* Erfarenhet från reglerade branscher (t.ex. finans eller bank).
* Erfarenhet från elbranschen.
* Erfarenhet av kvalificerat arbete med säkerhetsskydd inom myndighet, statligt bolag eller annan säkerhetskänslig verksamhet.
* Kunskap om säkerhetsskyddslagen, säkerhetsskyddsförordningen och Myndigheten för civilt försvar:s (MCF:s) föreskrifter.

**Bra att veta**
----------------


* Tjänsten är placerad i Sundbyberg.
* Viss möjlighet till distansarbete, upp till 50% när arbetsuppgifterna så tillåter.
* Resor förekommer i mycket begränsad omfattning i denna tjänst.
* Sista ansökningsdag 16 april 2026. Vi tar inte emot personligt brev i samband med din ansökan.
* Tjänsten är en tillsvidaretjänst med sex månaders provanställning.
* Förmåner | Svenska kraftnät


För mer information kontakta gärna följande personer



Rekryterande chef Josefin Agelii-Mayotte, tel. 010-350 94 88.  

Ansvarig rekryterare Lennart Jansson, tel. 010-350 91 49.
  

Fackliga representanter;  

Annika Ingeborn, SACO, tel. 010-475 87 72  

Erika Midfjäll, ST, tel. 010-475 87 31.
  

Du når oss också via e-post: fornamn.efternamn@svk.se.



Jobba här – bli en viktig del av vår energiframtid | Svenska kraftnät



Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt säljare i samband med rekrytering, vi har upphandlade avtal som gäller för Svenska kraftnät som statlig myndighet.

**Övrig information**
---------------------


Vi ställer höga krav på våra medarbetares säkerhets- och sekretessmedvetenhet. Innan ett beslut om anställning fattas kommer en säkerhetsprövning enligt säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585) att genomföras i de fall befattningen innebär hantering av säkerhetsskyddsklassificerade uppgifter eller deltagande i säkerhetskänslig verksamhet. Om anställningen avser befattning i säkerhetsklass kommer säkerhetsprövningen även att innefatta en registerkontroll och, i vissa fall, en särskild personutredning. För anställning på Svenska kraftnät kan det vara ett krav på svenskt medborgarskap i vissa befattningar. Med anställningen följer en skyldighet att krigsplaceras. Svenska kraftnät arbetar för att främja mångfald och motverka diskriminering och ser gärna sökanden med olika bakgrund och erfarenheter till våra rekryteringar.

title: Data Warehouse Engineer
description:
**Build the Future of Technology with Professional Galaxy AB**


Join a network of talented engineers, developers, cloud specialists, and AI innovators working on impactful projects across Sweden and Europe. At Professional Galaxy AB, we connect top tech talent with companies driving digital transformation in areas like cloud computing, software engineering, data, cybersecurity, and artificial intelligence.

  

Explore exciting opportunities and grow your career while working with cutting-edge technologies and forward-thinking teams.

 **About the Role: Data Warehouse Engineer**


We are looking for a Data Warehouse Engineer to design, develop, and maintain robust data warehouse solutions and ETL pipelines that power critical business insights. In this role, you will be a key contributor to building scalable data architectures that support data-driven decision-making.

 **Key Responsibilities:**

  

Design and implement complex ETL processes using SSIS.

  

Develop and optimize robust data warehouse solutions.

  

Manage and maintain MSSQL databases to ensure high performance.

  

Collaborate with stakeholders to transform complex information into actionable business insights.

  

Your Profile (Level 3: 4–8 Years Experience)


To be successful in this assignment, you should have a strong background in data engineering and a problem-solving mindset.

 **What We’re Looking For:**

  

Experience: Several years of hands-on experience in Data Warehouse development, including SSIS ETL and SQL Server.

  

Programming: Strong skills in T-SQL and C#.

  

Database Expertise: Deep expertise with MSSQL databases.

  

Architecture: A thorough understanding of Data Warehouse principles and design patterns.

  

Soft Skills: Excellent analytical abilities, a strong sense of ownership, and effective communication skills.

  

Languages: Fluency in English (written and spoken). Swedish is a significant plus.

 **Nice to Have:**

  

Experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) data applications.

  

Python programming skills.

  

Familiarity with BI tools such as Tableau, QlikView, or Looker.

  

Knowledge of test automation and Agile methodologies (SCRUM/SAFe).

 **Assignment Details**

**Start Date**: 2026-04-07

**End Date**: 2026-12-31 (with possibility of extension)

**Utilization**: 100%

**Offer Due Date**: 2026-04-07

 **How to Apply**


Are you the right person for the assignment, or do you want to recommend a strong candidate? Please apply directly through the Professional Galaxy AB portal with:

  

Your updated CV.

  

Your availability to start the assignment.

  

A motivation statement describing why you are suitable for this role—referencing previous consulting assignments, education, and personal qualities.

  

Please Note: We do not accept applications via email. All applications must be submitted through the portal to be valid.

  

Continuous Selection: We apply a continuous selection process. This means we may close the assignment before the deadline if the right candidate is found. We recommend that you apply immediately.

title: DevSecOps Engineer
description:
**About Intric**
----------------


Intric is a secure and sovereign AI platform that helps enterprises and government organizations build AI agents, automate workflows, and govern AI adoption. We are accelerating our growth, driven by a fresh round of funding and strong demand from both existing and new customers. We work with 70+ organizations across the public and private sector, and we're growing fast.


Security isn't a feature at Intric — it's foundational to everything we do. That's why we're looking for our first DevSecOps Engineer to own it.

**About the Role**
------------------


As DevSecOps Engineer, you will own the security and compliance posture of Intric's technical infrastructure. This means you're not just reacting to issues, but you're building the systems, processes, and configurations that ensure we're always compliant and never compromised.


You'll work closely with our infrastructure and engineering teams to embed security into the development lifecycle from day one, and you'll be the person who makes sure our configurations are locked down, auditable, and always up to date.

**What You’ll Do**
------------------

**You will play a key role in making sure** **that Intric's infrastructure is always secure, compliant, and trusted****.**


This includes:

* Own and maintain all security-related infrastructure configurations across our tech stack
* Ensure continuous compliance with relevant security standards and frameworks (e.g. ISO 27001, BSI C5, GDPR)
* Embed security practices into CI/CD pipelines and development workflows
* Conduct regular security audits, vulnerability assessments, and threat modeling
* Define and enforce policies for access control, secrets management, and network security
* Monitor infrastructure for anomalies and respond to security incidents
* Act as the internal authority on security best practices and educate the team accordingly

**Who You Are**
---------------

**We are less focused on tenure and more interested in potential and mindset.**

* 3–5 years of experience in DevSecOps, cloud security, or a similar role
* Solid understanding of infrastructure security in cloud environments
* Hands-on experience with Kubernetes and container security
* Familiarity with compliance frameworks such as ISO 27001, BSI C5, or equivalent
* Experience with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Helm, or similar)
* Strong understanding of secrets management, IAM, and network security principles
* A structured, ownership-driven mindset — you don't wait for someone to tell you something is broken

**Why Join Intric**
-------------------

* You'll own a critical function at a company where security is a core product promise
* Stock options and competitive salary
* Small team, real ownership: your decisions ship and your ideas get heard
* We're building the AI infrastructure for European enterprises and governments — the stakes are real

**Practical Information**
-------------------------

**Location:** Stockholm

**Language requirements:** Fluent in English

**Apply**
---------


We review applications continuously and look forward to hearing from you.

title: DevSecOps Engineer
description:
Join our growing Security Engineering team as a DevSecOps Engineer, where you'll take ownership of security tooling, automation, and infrastructure hardening, not just for the Stillfront Group, but also helping our 20+ game studios across the world. In this role, you won’t just shift left — you’ll sit at the heart of security operations, helping to design, implement, and manage the systems that protect our platforms at scale.


This role is to be located in our city center office in Stockholm Sweden.

 **YOUR MISSION**

* Own and Operate Security Tooling: Deploy, configure, and maintain security tools and platforms (e.g. vulnerability scanners, email protection, endpoint protection, SSO, SAST/DAST tools, secrets managers, container security platforms).
* Automate Security Operations: Build automation around security workflows, alert triage, and tool integrations using Python, Bash, Terraform, or similar tools.
* Vulnerability Management: Manage vulnerability detection tools, run or facilitate penetration tests, test impact from real exploits, support remediation workflows, and help teams prioritize risks based on real-world impact.
* Secure the Infrastructure: Collaborate with platform and cloud engineering teams to ensure secure configurations across AWS/GCP/Azure environments, including networking, IAM, logging, and encryption.
* Monitoring and Threat Detection: Support detection engineering efforts by ensuring telemetry from cloud services, containers, and endpoints flows reliably into monitoring platforms.
* Access & Secrets Management: Help design and maintain secure identity and access practices, including secrets management solutions.

 **YOUR PROFILE**

* 2–4+ years of experience in a security engineering, DevSecOps, or infrastructure security role, ideally within cloud-native environments.
* Strong knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP), including security services (IAM, KMS, VPC security, CloudTrail, etc.) and best practices for securing workloads.
* Hands-on experience managing security tools
* Strong scripting and automation skills in Python, Bash, or Go to build integrations, automate tasks, and customize tooling.
* Understanding of core security principles, including least privilege, zero trust, defense in depth, and threat modeling.
* Great communication and collaboration skills — you’ll work closely with local engineers as well as game developers at the studios.

 **NICE-TO-HAVE QUALIFICATIONS**

* Relevant Certifications such as AWS Certified Security – Specialty, GIAC (e.g., GSEC, GCSA), or more general security certification such as Security+, OSCP or CISSP, etc.
* Contributions to open-source security projects or active participation in the security community (e.g., blog posts, talks, GitHub contributions).
* Experience in threat detection or detection engineering, including writing custom detection rules or working with MITRE ATT&CK framework.
* Ability to assess and prioritize risk, translate technical issues into business impact, and make pragmatic security decisions.

##### **Stillfront**


Stillfront is a global gaming company. We develop a wide range of digital games that attract over 50 million players each month. From well-established franchises like Supremacy, Big Farm, and BitLife to niche games, we span many different genres, including strategy, simulation, RPG and action, and casual and mash-up games.


We believe gaming can be a force for good. And we want to create a gaming universe that is digital, affordable, equal, and sustainable. So, we focus on developing games that are all about having a rewarding hobby, a great social experience, or a strategic challenge.


Our HQ is in Stockholm, Sweden, but our game development is done by teams and studios all over the world. Our main markets are the US, Japan, MENA, Germany, and the UK. We’re proud that our company shares are listed on the Nasdaq Stockholm.

title: System Architect – Infrastructure & Tooling Team Lead
description:
**ABOUT THE COMPANY**


A specialist in identity, secure transactions, and digital services, IN Groupe is the global leader in secure identity and trust services. Every day, our 4,000 employees across 40 countries work to ensure that everyone has access to a reliable and secure identity, both in the physical and digital worlds. With our solutions and our international network of 10 R&D centers, we support governments and businesses in more than 130 countries.


Joining IN Groupe means helping to shape the future of the identity and secure transaction market. Become part of our international teams — driven by innovation and proud to design solutions that make life easier and protect personal data for millions of people around the world.

**ABOUT THE ROLE**


As **System Architect,** you will act as the technical lead for the **Infrastructure & Tooling** team in day-to-day operational matters while also owning the architectural direction of our SaaS service platforms.


You will work at the intersection of **operations and architecture** — ensuring stability and performance today, while designing scalable, secure, and automated platforms for tomorrow.


This role requires deep **technical expertise,** strong **leadership capabilities,** and the ability to balance operational excellence with long-term architectural thinking.

**MAIN RESPONSABILITIES**

* Act as **team lead** for operational, day-to-day infrastructure topics and staffing related tasks.
* Define and evolve the architecture of our **SaaS service platforms based on the IT OPS guidelines**
* Drive infrastructure automation and Infrastructure-as-Code initiatives
* Ensure high availability, scalability, and security across our platforms
* Design and maintain **Linux-based environments** (primarily RHEL-based distributions)
* Collaborate with development, security, and product teams to align platform capabilities with business needs
* Contribute to continuous improvement of operational processes, reliability, and resilience
* Support strategic decisions regarding infrastructure technologies and roadmap
 **ABOUT YOU**

* Very strong proficiency in **Ansible and Terraform**
* Solid experience with OpenStack (or similar private cloud platforms)
* Advanced knowledge of Linux environments (RHEL-based)
* Strong understanding of:
* Server infrastructure
* Storage solutions
* Networking
* Hardware Security Modules (HSMs)
* Experience designing and operating production-grade SaaS infrastructure
* Prior experience managing a team
* Comfortable working in security-sensitive and compliance-driven environments
* Professional proficiency in **Swedish and English**

**Nice-to-Have**

* Experience from regulated industries (eID, PKI, digital identity, financial services, public sector)
* Experience with high-availability architectures and geo-redundant designs
* Experience leading or mentoring other engineers
**Personal Qualities**

* A natural technical leader who enjoys guiding others in operational excellence
* Structured and analytical, with strong problem-solving skills
* Comfortable taking ownership of both daily operations and long-term strategy
* Passionate about automation, security, and resilient system design
* A team player who communicates clearly and collaborates across functions
**Background check**


Transparency, security, and trust are the core of our company; therefore, the final candidate will be subjected to a background check as a mandatory step in the recruitment process.

title: iOS Engineer – UI / Application Layer
description:
**Start date:** ASAP  

**Scope:** Full-time  

**Location:** Malmö (5 days onsite)

**Assignment Description**


An international organization is seeking an iOS Engineer specializing in the application layer, user interface, and the interaction between client applications and background services. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys crafting elegant user experiences while maintaining a strong understanding of how underlying systems interact.


The assignment is a consulting engagement where you will be employed by **Zcelero AB** and work on-site with one of Zcelero’s clients in Malmö.


You will work on building intuitive, high-quality user interfaces and ensuring seamless interaction between the UI layer and underlying services. The role requires a strong focus on clean architecture, maintainable code, and performance, while contributing to a consistent and engaging user experience.


You will collaborate closely with designers, engineers, and other stakeholders, contributing to both technical decisions and the evolution of the user experience.

**Key Responsibilities**


Develop and maintain user-facing features and application layer components within iOS


Design and implement clean, scalable, and maintainable UI architectures


Ensure smooth interaction between UI components and underlying services


Work with asynchronous flows and state management in modern iOS applications


Maintain high standards of code quality, testability, and performance


Collaborate with designers and developers to deliver high-quality user experiences


Contribute to continuous improvement of development practices and codebase

**Requirements (Must-have)**

* Several years of experience in iOS development (SwiftUI, UIKit, Objective-C, or Swift)
* Strong understanding of iOS architecture, data flow, and component structure
* Ability to write clean, testable, and maintainable code
* Good understanding of algorithms and fundamental software development concepts
* Basic understanding of asynchronous and concurrent programming
* Strong communication skills and fluency in English
* A strong interest in user experience and modern application development within mobile platforms

**Nice to Have**

* Experience with SwiftUI and modern UI patterns (e.g. MVVM, Combine)
* Knowledge of animation, accessibility, and performance optimization
* Understanding of interaction between UI and background processes
* Experience with build and test automation (CI/CD, Xcode Cloud)


We look forward to receiving your application!

title: Critical Environment Program Manager
description:
**Overview**  

Microsoft’s Cloud Operations & Innovation (CO+I) is the engine that powers our cloud services. As a CO+I Critical Environment Program Manager, you will perform a key role in delivering the core infrastructure and foundational technologies for Microsoft's online services including Bing, Office 365, Xbox, OneDrive, and the Microsoft Azure platform. As a group, CO+I is focused on the personal and professional development of all employees and offers trainings and opportunities including Career Rotation Programs, Diversity & Inclusion trainings and events, and professional certifications.


Our infrastructure is comprised of a large global portfolio of more than 200 datacenters in 32 countries and millions of servers. Our foundation is built upon and managed by a team of subject matter experts working to support services for more than 1 billion customers and 20 million businesses in over 90 countries worldwide.


With environmental sustainability and optimization at the forefront of our datacenter design and operations, we continue to grow and evolve as we meet the ever-changing business demands that hold Microsoft as a world-class cloud provider.


Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.

 **Responsibilities** ***Data Center Operations / Service Delivery:***

* Establish strong working relationships with customers.
* Oversee the day-to-day operations and maintenance of mechanical and electrical equipment in a datacenter
	+ Manage, plan control and organize the planned preventative and corrective electrical and/or mechanical equipment.
	+ This involves utilizing CMMS and monitoring the performance of electrical plant and machinery, ensuring work is undertaken to prevent failure/loss of the plant and equipment and the production of work order and allocating the work daily to the Technicians, monitoring progress, and inspecting completed work to ensure the required standards are achieved.
* Ensure that electrical, mechanical, and/or fire/life safety equipment within the datacenter is operating at peak efficiency.
* Ensure compliance with infrastructure operations standardization
* Act as an escalation point for all facilities-related issues within the datacenter, escalating to the Data Operations Manager as needed. This may include assisting technicians in times of emergencies, job training, and providing input/recommendations on electrical/mechanical design parameters.
* Management of small-to-medium projects from conception to completion
* Ensure routine reporting for critical infrastructure, inclusive of operational and maintenance KPIs, objectives, organizational polices, procedures, and standards.
* Driving cost/energy efficiency projects
* Identifying single points-of-failure at regional datacenter locations
* Coordinate, plan, schedule, and supervise CE audits and compliance verifications
* Drive identification and remediation of vulnerabilities (such as single points of failure). Provide recommendations on new data center equipment designs, technologies, and construction methods. Drive cost/energy efficiency projects

***Data Center Work Environment:***

* Share best practices, assists others in learning role, process, procedures; and mentors others
* Promotes a culture of safety, security, and compliance in all aspect of datacenter activities
* Realize the impact of change on others
* Learn, live, and coach the One Microsoft culture and values. Lead through change by bringing clarity, generating energy, and delivering success
* Removes barriers to agility to enable the team to shift priorities quickly without losing productivity.
* Embody our culture and values

  

**Qualifications**  

**Required Qualifications:**

* High School Diploma or equivalent AND **Good** year(s) of industry experience
	+ OR **Good** years industry experience in Datacenter or Cirtical Environment and college course work.

**Background Check Requirements:**


Ability to meet Microsoft, customer and/or government security screening requirements are required for this role. These requirements include, but are not limited to the following specialized security screenings:

* Microsoft Cloud Background Check: This position will be required to pass the Microsoft Cloud background check upon hire/transfer and every two years thereafter.


While not required, we also look for the following **Preferred Qualifications:**

* **Good** years of experience in Critical Environment infrastructures (e.g., UPS, Generator, AHU) or working in physical IT infrastructures (e.g., Servers, SANs, Networking, Capacity, DC Rack/Enclosures, structured cabling).
* Technical College degree in Computer Science, Math, Telecommunications, Electrical/Mechanical Engineering or related field.
* Applicable certifications: ASICS/Inventory Control, CompTIA, Microsoft, Network Certifications, PMP, ITIL, CDCP.
* Good experience working with the operation of building cooling, electrical, mechanical, and life safety systems
* Good experience with Electrical Power Monitoring Systems (EPMS), Branch Circuit Monitoring (BCM), and Building Automation Systems (BAS)
* Good years of experience with project management or relevant certification/training in project management
* Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree in applicable engineering field or mechanical or electrical trades
* Good years of experience working on large scale projects.

  

  

This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

  

Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process**.**
